skill.name: calculator
skill.description: Evaluates arithmetic expressions with arbitrary-precision decimal math for addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, parentheses, and integer exponents.
skill.summary: Calculate precise decimal arithmetic expressions without common floating-point errors.
skill.author: Code-and-Sorts

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported high-risk patterns, but review found the execution, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and network hits are false positives. The implementation is a local arithmetic parser using big.js, with low residual risk from local script execution and unbounded expression complexity.

low_findings.title: Unbounded Arithmetic Input Can Consume Resources
low_findings.description: The evaluator accepts an expression string and computes powers using big.js. Very long expressions or very large integer exponents could consume excessive CPU or memory, but there is no evidence of malicious intent or data access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly evaluates caller-provided expressions and exponentiation can grow quickly. The risk is limited to availability because the parser does not execute arbitrary code or access external resources.

low_findings.title: Local Script Commands Require Normal User Caution
low_findings.description: The usage instructions tell users to run npm install, build, and calculate commands. These are expected for a Node.js calculator skill, but marketplace users should review community scripts before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are plainly documented setup and execution steps. They are not hidden execution, but they still require user-controlled local command execution.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Findings Dismissed as False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Python exec and process execution findings are RegExp.exec calls, the dynamic import finding is a TypeScript type annotation, the weak-crypto findings are Jest descriptions or arithmetic notation, and the URL is a big.js documentation link.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review matched the flagged lines to benign TypeScript, Jest, Markdown, and documentation patterns. No command injection, cryptographic use, system reconnaissance, or outbound request behavior was found at those locations.

content.value_statement: Decimal calculations can produce wrong results when floating-point rounding matters. This skill evaluates arithmetic expressions with arbitrary-precision decimal math.
actual_capabilities: Evaluates addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division expressions.
Handles parentheses and standard operator precedence.
Calculates integer exponents with right-associative behavior.
Supports decimal values and negative numbers.
Uses big.js to avoid common binary floating-point errors.
Returns concise numeric results for command-line calculations.
limitations: Does not support trigonometric, logarithmic, or symbolic math.
Does not support variables, assignments, factorials, modulo, or bitwise operators.
Requires Node.js and dependency setup before local use.
Very large expressions or exponents may be slow or memory intensive.

use_cases.title: Check Decimal Calculations
use_cases.description: Verify totals, rates, and ratios where exact decimal arithmetic matters.

use_cases.title: Validate Expression Results
use_cases.description: Confirm arithmetic examples, tests, and documentation without JavaScript floating-point surprises.

use_cases.title: Practice Operator Precedence
use_cases.description: Evaluate expressions with parentheses, negative numbers, and exponents.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Calculation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the calculator skill to calculate 18.75 + 6.25.

prompt_templates.title: Precedence Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the calculator skill to evaluate 2 + 3 * 4 and explain the result briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Decimal Precision
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the calculator skill to calculate 0.1 + 0.2 and return the exact decimal result.

prompt_templates.title: Nested Expression
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the calculator skill to evaluate (2 + 3) * 4 ^ 2 - 10 / 5.

output_examples.output: The result is 512 because exponentiation is right-associative.

best_practices: Use clear arithmetic expressions with numbers, operators, and parentheses.
Keep inputs reasonably sized when calculating powers or long expressions.
Use this skill when decimal precision is more important than advanced math functions.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for symbolic algebra or variable substitution.
Do not use it for trigonometric, logarithmic, modulo, or bitwise operations.
Do not pass untrusted large expressions without resource limits.

faq.question: What does this skill calculate?
faq.answer: It calculates arithmetic expressions with addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, parentheses, decimals, negative numbers, and integer exponents.

faq.question: Why use this instead of normal JavaScript math?
faq.answer: It uses decimal arithmetic through big.js, so results like 0.1 + 0.2 return 0.3.

faq.question: Does it support advanced math functions?
faq.answer: No. It does not support trigonometric, logarithmic, symbolic, modulo, factorial, or bitwise operations.

faq.question: Does it execute arbitrary code?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed parser accepts only numbers, arithmetic operators, and parentheses.

faq.question: Does it send calculations over the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found in the reviewed calculator code. Setup commands may contact package registries when dependencies are installed.

faq.question: What should users watch for?
faq.answer: Avoid very large expressions or exponents unless the runtime has suitable CPU and memory limits.
